audience_flow_rules: Keep a clear entrance path to the seating area.
Keep service paths around banquet tables for catering staff.
Leave a visible processional aisle when ceremony language or bridal party cues are present.
Place the bridal table or stage at the visual focus of the room.
visibility_rules: Guests should be able to see the bridal table, stage, or podium without looking through service zones.
Avoid placing tall decor between guest tables and the stage.
Screens should be near the stage or side walls for speeches and livestream relay.
camera_placement_best_practices: Use a rear center camera for wide coverage.
Use side cameras near front corners for vows, speeches, and reactions.
Keep camera paths outside guest circulation paths.
Place an AV desk with line of sight to stage and screens when livestream is requested.
safety_accessibility: Do not block emergency exits.
Maintain accessible routes from entrance to seating, toilets, and exits.
Avoid cable runs across main aisles unless protected.
Keep dance floor and stage edges clear.

audience_flow_rules: Place registration near the entrance without blocking the door.
Create clear aisles from entrance to seating and stage.
Separate attendee circulation from presenter and AV work areas.
visibility_rules: Chairs face the podium and screen.
Use theatre seating for short talks and classroom seating for note-taking.
Keep screens high and centered or duplicated on sides for large rooms.
camera_placement_best_practices: Use rear center camera for speaker and slide context.
Use front side camera for speaker close-up.
Place AV desk at rear or side rear with screen and stage sightline.
safety_accessibility: Keep aisles continuous.
Do not place registration queues across emergency exits.
Provide wheelchair positions on aisle edges.

audience_flow_rules: Keep a center aisle for procession and movement.
Keep altar or stage as the primary focus.
Place AV control at rear or side rear.
visibility_rules: All rows face the altar or stage.
Screens should not compete with the altar focus.
Camera tripods should not block congregants.
camera_placement_best_practices: Use rear center camera for wide congregation and altar view.
Use side camera for pulpit close-up.
Keep cameras outside worship movement paths.
safety_accessibility: Keep communion, processional, and emergency paths clear.
Protect cables from cameras and AV desk.

audience_flow_rules: Create a quiet, direct entry path.
Keep a dignified central aisle toward the memorial area.
Avoid congesting the front memorial area.
visibility_rules: All seating should face the podium and memorial area.
Screens should support tribute media without dominating the memorial.
camera_placement_best_practices: Use discreet rear camera placement.
Use side camera only if it does not intrude on family seating.
Place AV desk away from family and ceremonial movement paths.
safety_accessibility: Reserve accessible seating near aisles.
Keep flower displays clear of exits and walkways.

audience_flow_rules: Place registration at entry with queue space.
Keep demo or product areas reachable without crossing stage operations.
Separate VIP or speaker movement from general circulation.
visibility_rules: Prioritize screen and speaker visibility.
Use cabaret when guests need tables while watching a stage.
camera_placement_best_practices: Rear center camera for wide stage and audience.
Front side camera for presenter close-up.
Side camera for product reveal or demo counter.
safety_accessibility: Keep branded displays outside exit paths.
Protect AV and demo cables.

audience_flow_rules: Keep entry, exit, and front-of-house routes separated where possible.
Preserve a clear route to the stage for crew.
visibility_rules: Stage is the dominant focus.
Avoid blocking audience sightlines with cameras or AV towers.
camera_placement_best_practices: Rear long-lens camera for full stage.
Side cameras for performers.
Keep camera lane clear of audience flow.
safety_accessibility: Keep exit routes wide and direct.
Protect high-traffic cable runs.

audience_flow_rules: Keep paths clear between tent entrances, seating, catering, and restrooms.
Place stage and AV away from weather exposure when possible.
visibility_rules: Account for tent poles and outdoor obstructions.
Keep cameras out of direct guest pathways.
camera_placement_best_practices: Use stable rear wide camera.
Use side cameras under weather cover.
Keep AV desk protected and close to power.
safety_accessibility: Do not block tent exits.
Avoid uncovered cables across grass or high-traffic routes.
Maintain accessible paths over uneven surfaces.

audience_flow_rules: Design loops so visitors can circulate past all booths.
Keep registration queue outside the main booth aisle.
visibility_rules: Entrance should reveal the main aisle and high-value booths.
Demo stages need clear standing room and sightlines.
camera_placement_best_practices: Use side/rear camera for demo stage.
Keep roaming camera paths out of queues.
safety_accessibility: Keep booth counters out of exit routes.
Avoid cable crossings in public aisles.

audience_flow_rules: Keep a processional route from entrance to stage.
Separate graduate movement from guest seating where possible.
visibility_rules: All guests should face stage and podium.
Screens help large rooms see certificate handover.
camera_placement_best_practices: Rear center camera for wide stage.
Side camera for certificate handover close-up.
Keep photo lane clear.
safety_accessibility: Keep processional aisle and exits clear.
Reserve accessible seating near aisles.

audience_flow_rules: Make the reveal area easy to see from entry and seating.
Keep press, VIP, and demo movement controlled.
visibility_rules: Product and screen are primary visual targets.
Avoid seating behind demo counters or camera rigs.
camera_placement_best_practices: Rear center camera for reveal and audience.
Side camera for product close-ups.
Optional roaming camera for audience reactions.
safety_accessibility: Protect demo power and AV cables.
Keep branding structures outside exits.
